one_liner: CMG up 3.3% on 2.96x volume (Selling Climax signal) — the 50-for-1 stock split two years ago means this $33 price = $1,667 pre-split; RSI 60.8 approaching overbought near $33.96 resistance, DCF shows 56% downside at $14.59.

price_action.intraday_story: CMG opened at $32.30, dipped to $32.26, then rallied to $33.59 — just above the BB upper at $33.96 — before closing at $33.34. The #1 volume day in the dataset (2.96x) with a close near the session high is bullish price action. However, the resistance is $33.96 (the BB upper), only $0.62 above the close — very limited remaining upside before hitting the band.

news_catalyst.headline: Two Years After Chipotle's 50-for-1 Stock Split — CAVA Up 37% in 6 Months Puts CMG's QSR Premium Under Scrutiny
news_catalyst.summary: The Chipotle split anniversary piece highlights that post-split CMG has underperformed its QSR peers. CAVA (the fast-casual challenger) is up 37% in 6 months while CMG has been flat-to-down. The implication: Chipotle's premium QSR multiple is under competition from newer, faster-growing concepts. CMG's DCF shows $14.59 intrinsic value — the stock needs to grow into its current multiple over many years of premium execution.

technical.summary: CMG at RSI 60.8 with the #1 volume day in its dataset is approaching the BB upper ($33.96) and the 90-day resistance ($35.78). Volume at 2.96x is climactic — the Selling Climax interpretation applies. MACD bullish crossover is constructive but the risk/reward is poor: $2.44 to resistance vs $4.40 to support.

wyckoff_narrative: The Selling Climax tag on the #1 volume day could mark a short-term floor if followed by an Automatic Rally. But without a prior downtrend, this is likely a standalone climactic volume event rather than a structural SC/AR/ST sequence.

valuation.summary: 56% DCF downside. Even the bull case at $27.45 is 18% below current price. CMG is the third most overvalued name in the watchlist (behind CSCO and DE).

key_strengths: Best-in-class QSR execution with digital ordering moat
Throughput improvement initiatives driving same-store sales
key_concerns: CAVA and other fast-casual competitors eroding the premium
fundamentals.summary: Grade C fundamentals — excellent business at a terrible price. CAVA's 37% rally while CMG flatlines is the market telling you which growth runway is more valuable.

risks: 56% downside to DCF base case — only 7% upside to 90-day resistance
RSI 60.8 approaching overbought near resistance — asymmetric risk/reward to the downside
CAVA's 37% gain in 6 months = relative underperformance narrative building
